library_preparation_preflight.summary: The artifact is a Scala 2.13 macro/stack-tracing support library; its POM marks Scala compiler/reflect APIs as provided, but its macro classes link to scala.reflect APIs, so meaningful coverage may require adding the Scala compiler to the test classpath.

deterministic_setup.reason:
zio-stacktracer_2.13 2.0.22 declares scala-compiler and scala-reflect as provided dependencies, and its macro implementation classes depend on scala.reflect.macros and related compiler/reflect APIs; adding scala-compiler also brings scala-reflect transitively for tests that exercise the macro-facing
...[truncated]

library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: Use self-contained tests; no Docker service or environment variable is indicated. Prefer exercising the runtime stack-tracer API directly and, if using Scala test sources to exercise macro expansion, keep assertions flexible for generated file names and line numbers because traces encode source location.
risks: The repository's generated scaffold and Gradle verification remain authoritative; if the scaffold already supplies Scala compiler support for _2.13 artifacts, the extra dependency may be redundant.
The library is mostly compile-time macro infrastructure, so Java-only tests can cover only the small runtime surface and may not reach the macro implementation paths.
